findings.one_line: This leaf is only an external citation to Le Cam/Bretagnolle-Huber and has no declared internal dependencies, so the testing bound is not reproduced on the present DAG.

The upper-rate proof relies on `lem:clipped-score-drift-bound`, `lem:clipped-score-variance-envelope`, `lem:feasible-master-bound`, and `lem:clip-balance-exponent`, but none of these are core nodes or declared dependencies, so the claimed exponent `r_feas` is not discharged from the visible DAG.

The localized-variance step is where `ass:clipped-propensity-variance-stability` would have to enter, but that assumption is neither stated in this node's headline hypotheses nor listed in `depends_on`, so the feasible upper rate is still conditional on a hidden premise.

The lower bound tracks the explicit witness construction, but the theorem's upper half is inherited entirely from the under-discharged node `oeq:feasible-upper`, so the advertised two-sided rate bracket is not yet proved on this core.
